summary: Sprint close-out review of: /voyager→/game redirect, VoyagerHomeStrip on Home, email open/click tracking pixel + redirect (Voita playbook), BackOfficePlaybook tracking columns, Voyager.jsx + BackOfficeMestariCopy.jsx refactors. Backend pytest tests/test_sprint_email_tracking.py = 10/10 PASS. Public-URL curl checks confirm /api/track/o/<token>.gif → 200 image/gif 43 B, /api/track/c/<token>?u=<base64 weezybet> → 302 Location=decoded, evil host → 400, /api/admin/playbook → outbox.tracking {outbox_total, opens_total, clicks_total}. Playwright verified /game renders all 7 testids (masthead/standfirst/game-block/review[0-3]/next[streamers,mestari]) with Pass+Redeem HIDDEN, /voyager redirects to /game, Home strip renders with all required testids and click navigates to /game, BackOfficePlaybook page exposes bo-pb-tracking-opens/clicks plus per-row bo-pb-row-tracking-<id>, BackOfficeMestariCopy auth-gates then renders FI/EN section panels (316 testids). Regression: /mestari /mittari /voita /peli /back-office all 200.

issues: Visual order pushes top of viewport into solid black band; standfirst headline only visible after scroll on a 1920×1080 viewport. Cosmetic, not a defect — content is present at correct testids.
CTA text rendered as 'OPEN PICK  →' (two spaces before arrow). Review spec writes single space — pure copy nit.

action_items: Naming note: review request referenced testid prefix 'mc-' (e.g. mc-save-top, mc-field-hero-fi-headline-lead) but the implemented prefix in BackOfficeMestariCopy + copyAtoms is 'mec-' (mec-save-top, mec-field-hero-fi-headline). Either rename the testids to match the documented contract or update the spec — current state works functionally but breaks any external test that follows the review's exact selectors.
Cosmetic: CTA copy in VoyagerHomeStrip has double space before the arrow ('OPEN PICK  →' / 'AVAA VALINTA  →').
critical_code_review_comments: Voyager.jsx refactor is clean — 103 LOC, single-purpose composition root with one effect for activeWeek and onWin scroll handler. Adapter for backend flat keys → nested {fi,en} is well-isolated.
VoyagerHomeStrip silently hides when /api/voyager/active returns nothing — good fail-closed default. Date math uses Math.ceil so 'NEXT PICK · 27 May · 7D' stays accurate even at midnight rollover.
/api/track/c/<token>?u=<base64> host-allowlist correctly rejects evil.example.com with 400 instead of redirecting; matches the spec.
copyAtoms extraction (SectionTitle/Field/Row/Card) reduced BackOfficeMestariCopy duplication but reintroduces a testid-prefix mismatch vs. the review brief (mec- vs mc-). Pick one and lock it.
BackOfficePlaybook tracking surface uses real /api/admin/playbook outbox.tracking shape — opens/clicks counters live-render even when value is 0, so QA can see the field exists pre-event.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing outbox rows (3) already in /api/admin/playbook.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All sprint deliverables verified at the public URL. test_sprint_email_tracking.py is green (10/10). The mc-/mec- testid prefix discrepancy is the only contract drift worth flagging — likely a documentation typo, not a regression. Mestari quiz flow itself was intentionally NOT re-tested (still validated in iter43).
